<!-- This template is based on The [Turing Way Bookdash Checklist](https://github.com/the-turing-way/the-turing-way/blob/main/.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/11_book_dash_checklist.md) -->

**The upcoming Event is from [yyyy-mm-dd] to [yyyy-mm-dd]**

This issue is for the event organisers, **if you are an [applicant of this event please use this form to submit your application: [add registration form link].** 

------------------------------------------------------------------

The following checklist is chronologically structured to help organise training events which are attended in *by application*, *online*. This checklist can be used as the primary issue to organise the event, with sub-issues created to document progress and decision making against each requirement as needed.

## Before any materials are prepared

### Contracts / Finance
- [ ] Agree RCM Co-Op invoice amount 
- [ ] Set up OLS budget template
- [ ] Add to RCM Coop Finance project board
- [ ] Confirm requirements/process for RCM Co-Op invoice payment
- [ ] Agree RCM Co-Op licensing terms of all materials CC-BY-4.0 (code MIT)
- [ ] Confirm if financial support avaialble to participants and how this will be diseminated

### Objectives
- [ ] Document Purpose and Values of all partners, including where aligned and driving this collaboration
- [ ] Define the *purpose* of the event
- [ ] Define the objectives based on the purpose
- [ ] Define how you will measure your success against objectives

### Ways of working
- [ ] Decide if there is route for participants or other interested parties to contribute to organising, and develop appropriate mechanisms or call to facilitate leadership contributions (consider adding to the application form)
- [ ] Confirm organising committee members
- [ ] Confirm the agreed communication channels for organising
- [ ] Confirm roles and responsibilities
- [ ] Confirm the agreed communication channels for partner interactions
- [ ] Document the decision-making process, including transparency and what information can be made public and what needs to remain private
- [ ] Document collaboration tech-stack and accessability requirements, including file storage, project management, materials preparations, online meeting tool
- [ ] Document meeting schedule, record of notes (inc meeting templates), allocation of actions, expectations for sync/async 
- [ ] Document methods of conflict resolution
- [ ] Document how feedback and learning from participants will be managed. 

## Before applications open

### Participants / advertising
- [ ] Define the desired attendee list (for invitation and appropriate advertising venues) to achieve the purpose
- [ ] Define application review rubric
- [ ] Publish participant application form, including accomodations for disability
- [ ] Write a short description for online advertisement
- [ ] Draft templates for short form advertising (e.g. for slack, social media) and 0.5-pager
- [ ] Set up an advertising webpage
- [ ] Open a call for participant applications
- [ ] Disseminate call and send direct invitations. Agree which channels to use and who has responsibility for targetting. 
- [ ] Close the application/registration round in time for review if required (minimum 1 month ahead of the event)
- [ ] Set-up a form for reviewing applications
- [ ] Set schedule for application reminders, including over which channels and who will be responsible for delivering

### Event activities
- [ ] Draft the agenda
- [ ] Confirm requirements for call hosts and ensure availability
- [ ] Finalise the event date and time

## After applications close
### Participants / advertising
- [ ] Review and score applications and comment on applications
- [ ] Send an email to the selected attendees
- [ ] Send meeting invites to selected participants with join link and links to materials
- [ ] Send an email to the unselected attendees with thanks and feedback

### Event activities
- [ ] Finalised agenda
- [ ] Create icebreaker activities
- [ ] Create slides
- [ ] Publish slides on zenodo (in RCM Cooperative Zenodo Community)

### Documentation
- [ ] Set up a Facilitators Briefing doc, for quick access to all links and resources to be shared during the event(s)
- [ ] Approve and publish Code of Conduct. Considder adding RCM Coop to reporting lines. 
- [ ] Decide mechanism for collecting and diseminating participant bios
- [ ] Create participants briefing pack with links to notes, agenda etc.
- [ ] Create video explainers to accompany briefing pack
- [ ] Email participants breifing pack
- [ ] Pin participants breifing pack to slack channel
- [ ] Remind of any prerequisites and resources to participte, e.g. Code of Conduct

### Tech-stack
- [ ] Ensure organising committee have access to all tech (slack, google drive, github)
- [ ] Set up an online meeting platform for remote participation
- [ ] Create space for in-event collaborative participant notes (e.g. google doc / hackmd)
- [ ] Create space for in-event participant handouts (e.g. miro)
- [ ] Set up a Slack channel for registered participants to welcome/introduce themselves. Model a welcome message. 
- [ ] Give attendees access to collaborative tools, e.g. slack, google drive, miro
- [ ] Set-up feedback forms
- [ ] Make sure all materials can be contibuted to async incase participants can't attend. Share links in advance where possible.
- [ ] Set up call recording

## During the event
- [ ] Follow facilitators breifing

## After the event
- [ ] Process and share call recordings
- [ ] Sanitise and share notes
- [ ] Develop a report with highlights and reccomendations
- [ ] Discuss the desire to reflect on the partnership with RCM coop (using Tried/Learnt/Pleased/Concerned/Do Next framework)
